# Test Fixtures

Hand-authored PNG fixtures for canonical-rights-metadata conformance tests (Plan 016).

## Provenance

All fixtures in this directory are hand-crafted XMP payloads embedded into minimal
1x1 RGB PNG files by the test harness in `canonical_rights.rs`. None are generated
by the stegoeggo library itself.

The XMP payloads follow the W3C RDF/XMP specification and use:

- PLUS namespace: `http://ns.useplus.org/ldf/xmp/1.0/`
- Controlled-vocabulary keys from the PLUS LDF standard
- Legacy IPTC DMI properties for backward-compatibility testing

## Fixture scenarios

| Scenario | XMP property form | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Canonical attribute | `plus:DataMining="DMI-..."` | Standard attribute on rdf:Description |
| Canonical element | `<plus:DataMining>DMI-...</plus:DataMining>` | Element child of rdf:Description |
| Alternate prefix | `myplus:DataMining="DMI-..."` | Arbitrary prefix bound to PLUS namespace |
| Legacy IPTC | `Iptc4xmpExt:DMI-Prohibited="ProhibitedAiMlTraining"` | v0.2-era StegoEggo format |
| Agreeing | Both canonical + legacy with same value | No conflict expected |
| Conflicting | Canonical + legacy with different values | Conflict expected |
| Unknown vocab | `plus:DataMining="DMI-UNKNOWN-VALUE"` | Unrecognized URI |
| Malformed | Broken XML | Parser must not panic |
| No metadata | Plain PNG, no XMP | Empty result expected |

## Why hand-authored?

The plan requires that fixtures not be generated by stegoeggo to avoid closed-loop
testing (where the writer and parser share the same internal representation).
Hand-crafting the XMP ensures the parser is tested against externally specified
payloads.
